title: Improve TUI keyboard shortcuts for tree navigation and detail sections
description:
Two keyboard shortcut improvements:

1) BEAD LIST NAVIGATION: The current shortcuts for top/bottom of the tree (Home, End, PageUp, PageDown) are unavailable on Apple keyboards without a full-size layout. Add alternative bindings: 'g' for go to top (first bead), 'G' (shift+g) for go to bottom (last bead). These are vim-style and intuitive. Keep the existing Home/End/PageUp/PageDown bindings for users who have them.

2) DETAIL PANEL SECTION NAVIGATION: The current shortcuts to cycle between collapsible sections in the detail panel use '[' and ']' which are awkward to reach. Replace with 'n' (next section) and 'N' (shift+n, previous section) when the detail panel is focused. Remove the '[' and ']' bindings. Update the help overlay to reflect the new bindings.
acceptance_criteria: g goes to the first bead in the list, G goes to the last
Detail section navigation uses a more accessible shortcut than [ and ]
Help overlay reflects all new and changed bindings

handoff_summary.completed: 1) Added g (go to top) and G (go to bottom) bindings for bead list navigation, mapped to existing go_home/go_end actions. 2) Replaced [ / ] with n (next) / N (previous) for detail section navigation. 3) Moved cancel_pending_action binding from n to c. 4) Updated help overlay text with new g/G, n/N, and c bindings. 5) Updated detail panel subtitle hint from [/] to n/N. 6) Updated test assertions: bracket key press → n key press, help overlay assertions for new bindings.
handoff_summary.risks: The cancel binding changed from n to c — users accustomed to pressing n to cancel will need to use c instead. This is a minor UX change needed to free up n for section navigation as requested.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run tester and documentation followup beads to validate changes and update any user-facing docs.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Tests in test_tui.py were updated to match new keybindings — any concurrent changes to TUI keybinding tests may conflict.

conflict_risks: Tests in test_tui.py were updated to match new keybindings — any concurrent changes to TUI keybinding tests may conflict.

Improved TUI keyboard shortcuts: added g/G vim-style bindings for jumping to first/last bead (alongside existing Home/End), replaced [ / ] detail section navigation with n/N (more accessible), reassigned cancel from n to c to resolve the conflict, and updated help overlay and detail panel subtitle to reflect all changes.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run only the specific test files directly related to your changes, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. Leave comprehensive test validation to the tester agent.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
